page: Amazon Web Services r5ad.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The system includes 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It is equipped with 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core performance is relatively low, with stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests scoring in the bottom 10%. Conversely, multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the middle 50% tier. Notably, the processor demonstrates strong cryptographic performance, scoring in the top 25% on the Passmark CPU encryption test.

The inclusion of high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage alongside a dense memory footprint provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high-speed temporary storage without the overhead of network-attached volumes. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and multi-threaded processing tasks, while it is less optimal for single-threaded applications or large-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 64 vCPUs, 32 physical AMD EPYC 7571 cores running at 2.5 GHz, and 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance features 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU metrics and single-threaded decompression are in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and Redis operations rank in the average middle 50% tier. Cryptographic processing is a relative strength, scoring in the top 25%. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive databases and multi-threaded workloads that benefit from local NVMe instance storage, though it is less efficient for single-threaded tasks or heavy LLM text generation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 2400 GB local NVMe storage, offering balanced multi-core performance and strong encryption capabilities.

tagline: A memory-optimized instance combining a dense DDR4 memory footprint with high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage for data-intensive workloads.

512.0 GB of DDR4 RAM with a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio
